briefdescription.para: rEndo = rScale * pow( surroundVol, rPower_)
aEndo = aScale * pow( surroundVol, aPower_)
vEndo = vScale * pow( surroundVol, vPower_)
EndoMeshes inside. Used to update.

detaileddescription.para: The reason why the powers don't have to be 1/3, 1/2 and 1 is because some organelles are not a simple linear vol relationship with their surround. I want to keep flexibility.

briefdescription.para: rEndo = rScale * pow( surroundVol, rPower_)

detaileddescription.para: Surrounding mesh. Use this for calculating all volume and diffusion terms, don't maintain any local variables.

briefdescription.para: aEndo = aScale * pow( surroundVol, aPower_)
vEndo = vScale * pow( surroundVol, vPower_)
Volume of mesh Entry including abutting diff-coupled voxels.

detaileddescription.para: Virtual function to return volume of mesh Entry, including for diffusively coupled voxels from other solvers.

briefdescription.para: Virtual function to return coords of mesh Entry.

detaileddescription.para: Virtual function to return coords of mesh Entry. For Endo mesh, coords are middle of parent followed by radius.

briefdescription.para: Virtual function to return diffusion X-section area.

detaileddescription.para: Virtual function to return diffusion X-section area for each neighbor This applies if we have endo mesh voxels diffusing with each other

briefdescription.para: Virtual function to return scale factor for diffusion. 1 here.

detaileddescription.para: Virtual function to return scale factor for diffusion. 1 here. This applies if we have endo mesh voxels diffusing with each other

briefdescription.para: Virtual function to return dimensions of specified entry.
Virtual function to return volume of mesh Entry.
Virtual function to return MeshType of specified entry.
Inherited virtual, do nothing for now.

detaileddescription.para: This means the sibling voxel immediately adjacent to it, not the voxel surrounding it. Should we wish to do diffusion we would need to use the parent voxel of the surround voxel. Otherewise use EMTPY_VOXEL == -1U.

memberdef.argsstring: (unsigned int index, double &x, double &y, double &z) const
memberdef.name: indexToSpace

detaileddescription.para: Converts specified index to xyz coords of middle of voxel Values out of range return original xyz

memberdef.argsstring: (const Eref &e, double volume, unsigned int numEntries)
memberdef.name: innerBuildDefaultMesh

briefdescription.para: Virtual func to make a mesh with specified Volume and numEntries.
Virtual function to return # of spatial dimensions of mesh.

detaileddescription.para: Inherited virtual func. Returns number of MeshEntry in array

memberdef.argsstring: (const Eref &e, unsigned int numNodes, unsigned int numThreads)
memberdef.name: innerHandleNodeInfo

memberdef.argsstring: (const Eref &e, const SrcFinfo2< unsigned int, vector< double > > *meshStatsFinfo)
memberdef.name: innerHandleRequestMeshStats

param.type: const SrcFinfo2< unsigned int, vector< double > > *

briefdescription.para: More inherited virtual funcs: request comes in for mesh stats.
Inherited virtual func.

detaileddescription.para: Inherited virtual func. Dummy, because the EndoMesh just does what its parent does.

Returns the meshIndices (NOT spatial indices) of all adjacent mesh entry pairs on ether side of the (self, other) junction. meshIndices are the indices that look up entries in the vector of pools. spatialIndices are (iz * ny + iy) * nx + ix, that is, a linear conversion of cartesian spatial indices. So, for two touching cubes, the return vector is the paired meshIndices on either side of the plane of contact. If one mesh has a finer mesh than the other, or if there are more than one contact points from self to other (for example, at a corner), then we just have multiple pairs using the same meshIndex of the repeated voxel.

inbodydescription.para: Consider concentric EndoMeshes in an outer cyl/sphere of radius r_0. Radii, from out to in, are r_0, r_1, r_2.... r_innermost. Consider that we are on EndoMesh n. The diffusion length in each case is 1/2(r_n-1 - r_n+1). For the innermost we use r_n+1 = 0 The diffusion XA is the area of the EndoMesh.

memberdef.argsstring: (double x, double y, double z, unsigned int &index) const
memberdef.name: nearest

detaileddescription.para: Returns distance and index of nearest mesh entry. Computes each mesh entry position as its geometric centre. If the current location is not inside a valid mesh entry, distance returned is negative.

briefdescription.para: Inherited virtual. Returns entire volume of compartment.
Virtual func so that derived classes can return voxel midpoint.
Virtual func so that derived classes can pass voxel volume back.
Inherited virtual. Resizes len and dia of each voxel.

detaileddescription.para: The EndoMesh is a cellular compartment entirely contained within another compartment. It supports diffusion only with its immediate surround, of which there can only be one, and with its immediate interior, which can be one or more EndoMeshes. Each voxel in the EndoMesh is well mixed, and does not exchange with any other voxels. Typically used in modelling endosomes, ER, mitochondria, and other organelles.
